title: Constitution of India
content: The President of India: There shall be a President of India who shall be the Head of State and shall exercise his powers directly or through officers subordinate to him in accordance with this Constitution.

title: Constitution of India
content: Executive power of the Union: The executive power of the Union shall be vested in the President and shall be exercised by him either directly or through officers subordinate to him in accordance with this Constitution.

title: Constitution of India
content: Election of President: The President shall be elected by the members of an electoral college consisting of the elected members of both Houses of Parliament and the elected members of the Legislative Assemblies of the States.

title: Constitution of India
content: Manner of election of President: As far as practicable, there shall be uniformity in the scale of representation of the different States at the election of the President. The election of the President shall be held in accordance with the system of proportional representation by means of the single transferable vote and the voting at such election shall be by secret ballot.

title: Constitution of India
content: Term of office of President: The President shall hold office for a term of five years from the date on which he enters upon his office. The President may resign his office by writing under his hand addressed to the Vice-President.

title: Constitution of India
content: Eligibility for re-election: A person who holds, or who has held, the office of President shall, subject to the other provisions of this Constitution, be eligible for re-election to that office.

title: Constitution of India
content: Qualifications for election as President: No person shall be eligible for election as President unless he is a citizen of India, has completed the age of thirty-five years, and is qualified for election as a member of the House of the People. A person shall not be eligible for election as President if he holds any office of profit under the Government of India or the Government of any State or under any local or other authority subject to the control of any of the said Governments.

title: Constitution of India
content: Conditions of President's office: The President shall not be a member of either House of Parliament or of a House of the Legislature of any State, and if a member of either House of Parliament or of a House of the Legislature of any State is elected President, he shall be deemed to have vacated his seat in that House on the date on which he enters upon his office as President. The President shall not hold any other office of profit. The President shall be entitled without payment of rent to the use of his official residences and shall also be entitled to such emoluments, allowances and privileges as may be determined by Parliament by law and, until provision in that behalf is so made, such emoluments, allowances and privileges as are specified in the Second Schedule.

title: Constitution of India
content: Oath or affirmation by the President: Before the President enters upon his office, he shall make and subscribe in the presence of the Chief Justice of India or, in his absence, the senior-most Judge of the Supreme Court available, an oath or affirmation in the following form: 'I, A.B., do swear in the name of God/solemnly affirm that I will faithfully execute the office of President of the Union of India and will to the best of my ability preserve, protect and defend the Constitution and the law and that I will devote myself to the service and well-being of the people of the Republic of India.'

title: Constitution of India
content: Procedure for impeachment of the President: When a President is to be impeached for violation of the Constitution, the charge shall be preferred by either House of Parliament. No such charge shall be preferred unless the proposal to prefer such charge is contained in a resolution which has been moved after at least fourteen days' notice in writing signed by not less than one-fourth of the total number of members of the House has been given of their intention to move the resolution.

title: Constitution of India
content: Time of holding election to fill vacancy in the office of President and the term of office of person elected to fill casual vacancy: An election to fill a vacancy caused by the expiration of the term of office of President shall be completed before the expiration of the term. If the office of President becomes vacant by reason of his death, resignation or removal, or otherwise, the election to fill the vacancy shall be held as soon as possible after, and in no case later than six months from, the date of occurrence of the vacancy.

title: Constitution of India
content: The Vice-President of India: There shall be a Vice-President of India.

title: Constitution of India
content: The Vice-President to be ex officio Chairman of the Council of States: The Vice-President shall be ex officio Chairman of the Council of States and shall not hold any other office of profit. The Vice-President shall receive such salary and allowances as may be determined by Parliament by law.

title: Constitution of India
content: The Vice-President to act as President or to discharge his functions during casual vacancies in the office, or during the absence, of President: In the event of the occurrence of any vacancy in the office of the President by reason of his death, resignation or removal, or otherwise, the Vice-President shall act as President until the date on which a new President elected in accordance with the provisions of this Chapter to fill such vacancy enters upon his office.
